title: Financial Stability Oversight Council Issues Proposed Guidance on Nonbank Financial Company Designations
summary: The Financial Stability Oversight Council voted unanimously on March 25, 2026 to issue proposed interpretative guidance on nonbank financial company designations, reestablishing elements from the 2019 guidance while adding enhancements that incorporate economic growth and economic security into the Council's financial stability analysis.
key_points: FSOC voted unanimously to issue proposed interpretive guidance on nonbank financial company designations for public comment
Guidance reestablishes elements from the 2019 interpretive guidance and adds enhancements reflecting current understanding of financial stability
Proposed guidance incorporates economic growth and economic security as factors in assessing risks to financial stability
Council prioritizes activities-based approach over entity-specific designations; entity designations only if activities-based approach is insufficient
Council commits to performing cost-benefit analysis before any designation decision
New pre-designation off-ramp allows companies or regulators to address identified risks before formal designation
45-day public comment period will follow Federal Register publication
why_it_matters: This regulatory guidance shapes how FSOC will designate systemically important nonbank financial companies. The shift toward an activities-based approach and the addition of cost-benefit analysis requirements could affect which institutions face heightened监管 scrutiny going forward. Market participants should monitor which activities or sectors might be flagged under the new framework.
macro_context: The proposed guidance explicitly incorporates economic growth and economic security into FSOC's analysis, signaling that regulatory actions will consider broader macroeconomic implications. Secretary Bessent stated this approach aims to prevent threats from translating into real economic harms.
